preamble: The loss of the retroflex lateral [ɭ], merging it into the dental lateral [l]. This is a characteristic of Modern Standard Hindi and many other Northern Indo-Aryan dialects.

examples.note: Merger of historical /ɭ/ into /l/ in standard Hindi.

sources: Masica, C. P. (1991). The Indo-Aryan Languages.
Fortson, B. W. (2010). Indo-European Language and Culture: An Introduction.
